_description: MAXIMUM DIFFICULTY — THE INVISIBLE ATTACK. No urgency language. No new account. No overt domain spoofing in the FROM address. Professional tone throughout. Every individual signal has a plausible innocent explanation. The attack only becomes visible when 5 data-layer signals are cross-referenced simultaneously: (1) FROM is billing@hawthorn-legal.com (legitimate) but the Return-Path buried in raw headers is billing@hawth0rn-legal.com — a zero replacing the letter o, the canonical BEC domain clone; (2) CC is the firm's managing partner Robert Ashford, listed in the vendor record as having RETIRED December 2025 — his name is used as a social trust anchor, not because he still works there; (3) payment terms changed from Net-45 to Net-30 with a vague 'updated billing policy' explanation that does not appear anywhere in the vendor record; (4) the invoice cover period is 'January 15 – February 28' — but the engagement letter specifies calendar-quarter billing (Jan 1 – Mar 31), and mid-January start dates have never appeared in prior invoices; (5) the invoice is submitted March 14 — Q1 does not end until March 31, meaning this retainer is being pushed for payment 17 days before the billing cycle even closes. Each signal alone: rationalizeable. Together: the Return-Path clone proves the FROM address is spoofed, the retired CC contact is a planted trust signal, the terms change and early submission are payment acceleration mechanics designed to force wire before anyone calls to verify. A solo model anchors on 'known domain, known account, professional tone, amount in range' and ALLOWS. Only the adversarial chain — where Turn 2 challenges Turn 1's Return-Path rationalization and Turn 3 connects that to the payment acceleration pattern — surfaces the full attack picture.

email_chain.subject: Hawthorn Legal Group — Q1 2026 Retainer Invoice HLG-2026-Q1-01
email_chain.body:
Dear Michelle,

Please find enclosed our Q1 2026 retainer invoice for legal services rendered January 15 through February 28, 2026 in connection with the Crestview Capital ongoing advisory and compliance matters.

Invoice number: HLG-2026-Q1-01
Engagement period: January 15 – February 28, 2026
Retainer amount: $52,000.00
Payment terms: Net-30 (per our updated billing policy)
Due date: April 13, 2026

Please remit to the wire instructions on file. No changes to banking details.

We appreciate the continued relationship and look forward to supporting Crestview Capital through Q2.

Warm regards,
Billing Administration
Hawthorn Legal Group LLP

vendor_record.billing_cycle: calendar quarter — invoices submitted after quarter close

known_contacts.name: Robert Ashford
known_contacts.title: Managing Partner
known_contacts.status: RETIRED — announced November 2025, final day December 31 2025

sender_history.all_prior_invoice_periods: always calendar quarters: Q1=Jan1-Mar31, Q2=Apr1-Jun30, Q3=Jul1-Sep30, Q4=Oct1-Dec31
sender_history.all_prior_payment_terms: Net-45 on every prior invoice without exception

context.org_policies: Wire transfers require Finance Director approval plus independent callback verification to a phone number sourced from the original vendor onboarding record — not from the invoice or email. Any change to payment terms from the signed engagement letter requires written consent from the General Counsel. Invoices must be cross-referenced against the vendor's stated billing cycle; invoices submitted before a billing cycle closes are to be held pending cycle close. The Return-Path header on all inbound vendor emails must match the FROM domain; mismatches are to be escalated to IT Security before any payment is processed.
